architectureSummary.reviewFocus: Full declared and observed module graph.

interpretation.headline: This scoped import graph is quiet: no hard failures, visible debt, advisory signals, or baseline drift were reported; graph center is CheckRunner, so compare that center with your intended architecture before saving a baseline. Quiet imports do not prove intra-file responsibilities are healthy.
quickRead: Contract: no hard failures in this command output.
Graph center: CheckRunner (20 import sites, fan-in 2, fan-out 7), CoreTypes (19 import sites, fan-in 6, fan-out 0).
Review pressure: no visible debt or advisory signals.
lookFirst: Hard signals: read `violations[]`, `intentionalDebt[]`, and advisory `warnings[]` before judging the diagram.
Graph center: inspect CheckRunner; it carries the strongest observed coupling in this scan.
Shape fit: compare central modules, deep imports, drift, and any intra-file pressure signals with the architecture you expected for this repository.
interpretation.caveat: This is a graph interpretation over static imports, not proof of semantic architecture health. Compare it with the architecture you intended.

reviewStory.summary: This scoped import graph is quiet. Confirm the graph center matches intended architecture before saving or updating a baseline; quiet imports do not prove intra-file responsibilities are healthy.
reviewStory.setup: Scanned 12 declared modules and 55 observed import edges. This report is advisory unless you run `axi check` as the gate.

pressures.title: Quiet graph center: CheckRunner
pressures.description: No hard failures, visible debt, advisory signals, or drift were reported; compare this center with the architecture you expected before saving a baseline.

reviewStory.nextStep: Confirm scan scope and intended graph shape, then save a portable baseline with `axi graph --json --portable` if this is the shape to watch.
reviewStory.caveat: This story is a review aid over static imports. It points to likely pressure, not proof that the architecture is good or bad; a quiet import graph can still hide intra-file responsibility concentration.

suggestedNextActions: Save an unfiltered portable `axi graph --json --portable` baseline if you want future PRs to show architecture drift.
